assertion_results.text: Step 0 detects the absence of the Deployment Context column in the Source Repositories table and defaults all repositories to upstream (§1.78)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 37-38 explicitly state: 'Source Repositories table does **not** have a Deployment Context column. Per backward compatibility rules, all repositories default to `upstream`.' The absence is detected and the default-to-upstream behavior is applied.

assertion_results.text: Remediation task descriptions do NOT include a Coordination Guidance subsection — the section is omitted entirely when no Deployment Context column exists
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md contains 4 tasks. None include a 'Coordination Guidance' subsection. Task description sections are: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Dependencies. data-extraction.md line 39 also explicitly confirms: 'Coordination Guidance subsection is omitted from remediation tasks.'

assertion_results.text: The triage proceeds normally through all steps without errors from the missing Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: Both output files are complete and well-formed. data-extraction.md includes all expected sections (CVE Metadata, Stream Scope Resolution, Ecosystem Detection, Deployment Context, Version Impact Table, Affects Versions Correction, Cross-Stream Impact). remediation.md produces 4 complete remediation tasks (2 for 2.2.x Case A, 2 for 2.1.x Case B). No error messages, warnings, or failure indicators appear in either file.

assertion_results.text: Backward compatibility is maintained — existing eval behavior is unaffected by the absence of the Deployment Context column
assertion_results.evidence: The triage output follows the expected structure: data extraction correctly identifies CVE metadata, performs version impact analysis across streams, corrects Affects Versions, detects cross-stream impact, and generates appropriate remediation tasks with proper Case A/Case B classification. The backward compatibility rule is explicitly referenced in data-extraction.md line 38 ('Per backward compatibility rules'). All standard workflow outputs are present and correctly structured.
